prompt:
You are auditing the following documented color pairs for a design system. One token plays a DUAL role across the screen specs in scope: it is used BOTH as the foreground for small body text (the 14px form-helper text under input fields) AND as the foreground for the form-input border. Both usages share the same background. Produce a pairwise contrast matrix per WCAG 2.2 AA and apply the multi-context scoring rule strictly.

Documented pairs (light theme only):

| Token foreground (hex) | Token background (hex) | Documented usages |
|---|---|---|
| `color.text.muted` (#888888) | `color.surface.background` (#FFFFFF) | (a) 14px form-helper body text, (b) form-input border |
| `color.text.primary` (#222222) | `color.surface.background` (#FFFFFF) | normal-text |

Known ratios:
- `#888888` on `#FFFFFF` ~ 3.54:1
- `#222222` on `#FFFFFF` ~ 15.9:1

WCAG target: AA. The `color.text.muted` token serves TWO design contexts (normal-text and ui-component) which carry DIFFERENT AA thresholds (4.5:1 and 3:1 respectively). Apply the strictest applicable threshold and produce: (1) contrast matrix with explicit design_context labeling (and a multi-context flag where applicable); (2) failing pair list with concrete remediation; (3) summary count.
expected_output: The `color.text.muted` pair at 3.54:1 MUST be scored against the STRICTEST applicable threshold (normal-text 4.5:1 AA), NOT the looser ui-component threshold (3:1) where it would PASS. The verdict is FAIL-AA because as small body text it fails. Persona must explicitly label it multi-context and note the dual usage. Remediation: darken color.text.muted to something like #6B6B6B (~5.33:1) so it passes BOTH contexts, OR split into two tokens (one for body text, one for borders). The discriminator: a baseline reviewer who picks the looser threshold (ui-component, 3:1) reports PASS and manufactures false confidence on form-helper text accessibility.
